steps.title: Eliminate y by substitution
steps.content:
From the first equation $x - y = 1$, express $y = x - 1$. Substitute into $x + y = x^2 - 3$:
$$x + (x - 1) = x^2 - 3$$
$$2x - 1 = x^2 - 3$$
$$x^2 - 2x - 2 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic and find the complete solutions
steps.content:
Apply the quadratic formula:
$$x = \frac{2 \pm \sqrt{4 + 8}}{2} = \frac{2 \pm 2\sqrt{3}}{2} = 1 \pm \sqrt{3}$$
For each x-value, use $y = x - 1$:
  • When $x = 1 + \sqrt{3}$: $y = \sqrt{3}$, giving $(1 + \sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3})$
  • When $x = 1 - \sqrt{3}$: $y = -\sqrt{3}$, giving $(1 - \sqrt{3}, -\sqrt{3})$
The solution $(1 + \sqrt{3}, \sqrt{3})$ matches choice A — A is correct.
